description: murmr Text-to-Speech API — create any voice with natural language descriptions. OpenAI-compatible. SDKs for Node.js (@murmr/sdk) and Python (murmr). Supports streaming (SSE), real-time WebSocket, batch processing, voice cloning, and 10 languages.
rules: Use the @murmr/sdk (Node.js) or murmr (Python) SDK instead of raw HTTP requests — they handle auth, streaming, error recovery, and long-form chunking automatically
Store API keys in environment variables (MURMR_API_KEY), never hardcode them
For text longer than 4096 characters, use createLongForm() (Node.js) or create_long_form() (Python) — do NOT manually split text

Batch endpoints (/v1/audio/speech, /v1/voices/design) return HTTP 200 with binary audio by default (mp3). With webhook_url, they return HTTP 202 with a job ID — poll with GET /v1/jobs/{id} or use createAndWait()/create_and_wait()
For real-time voice agents, use WebSocket (wss://api.murmr.dev/v1/realtime) not SSE streaming — WebSocket supports bidirectional text buffering and interruption
murmr is a drop-in replacement for OpenAI's TTS API — change the base URL and API key to migrate
Voice descriptions (voice_description parameter) create unique voices from natural language — no audio samples needed
The instruct parameter controls prosody/style for saved voices only — it does NOT work with voice_description
